raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, my Citi credit card number was used to make 3 fraudulent purchases at XXXX online. Each purchase was for {$250.00}, for a total of {$770.00}. 

I noticed these charges on XX/XX/year>, and reported them to Citi as fraudulent purchases. Citi said they would refund the {$770.00}. 

Later, I learned that XXXX told Citi that the online order was picked up ( in XXXX ; I live in New York ), and therefore was a legitimate purchase. Citi provided me with the information that XXXX provided them. ( See attached file XXXX ) The purchase was made using a fake email address. The items, large boxes of flooring, were picked up in XXXX XXXX, where I do not live. 

Citi reversed their decision, and charged me the full {$770.00} again. They didn't contact me to tell me this, I discovered it myself later when my credit card statement seemed too high. 

I have since called Citi multiple times over the past 2 years, explaining that this was obviously a fraudulent purchase, based on the evidence they already have. Every time, they tell me that they have to re-open the account and re-start the investigation process. Citi assures me they have all the information they need ( which is true ), they can see it was a fraudulent purchase, and will contact me when the investigation is complete. 

Then, nothing happens. Multiple times, I have called Citi back a couple of months later, and they tell me the same thing again ; they have to re-open the account and start the investigation, etc... They can see it was a fraudulent charge and they will contact me when the investigation is complete, etc... 

I have been doing this every few months since 2022, when this first began. I have files the dispute forms Citi requited, and provided them with all the information they have asked for. I would like Citi to finally resolve my fraud dispute case and refund me the {$770.00} I am owed.
